  • Gallery

  • Changelog

Articles

Get started

Getting started

From a data frame to a rendered plot to an interactive widget, in one pipeline, using the whole vellum ecosystem through vellumverse.

The vellum ecosystem

How vellum, vellumplot, and vellumwidget fit together, from a low-level scene graph, to a grammar of graphics, to an interactive widget.

Coming from ggplot2

A translation guide for ggplot2 users: the same plots built both ways, side by side, and the handful of concepts that differ.

Using the ecosystem

One scene, three outputs

The ecosystem’s central move: a plot is compiled once into a vellum scene, then rendered to raster, to vector, or to an interactive widget from the same object.

Performance and big data

Why the ecosystem stays fast as data grows, and how datashade renders millions of points by aggregating before it draws.

Extending the ecosystem

The seams the three layers agree on, and how to build on them: composite grobs, custom themes, the interactivity contract, and teaching the ecosystem about your own objects.

Background

A critical appraisal of grid

What R’s grid graphics system does well, where it becomes hard to work with, and what the ecosystem of packages built on top of it reveals about its gaps. An even-handed look, not a takedown.

vellum's design principles

The architectural choices behind vellum (a retained scene graph, eager metrics, an explicit layout pass, and a single render walk to many backends), and the trade-offs each one accepts.

On this page

Developed by David Schoch.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.1.
